description: '''Grungegaze''' (also known as '''dreamo''') is a subgenre of [[soft grunge music]] that combines elements of [[shoegaze]] and [[grunge]]. It is characterized by the use of [[emo]]-inspired melodies and harmonies and [[dynamic (music)|dynamic]] songwriting, using both soft reverb-heavy guitars during verses and crescendos using fuzz for choruses. Soft grunge had been closely related to the [[Shoegaze#Developments in the hardcore scene|hardcore scene's 2010s shoegaze revival]], with both styles sharing musical characteristics and sometimes being conflated by fans and critics. Grungegaze developed during 2013, when soft grunge bands embraced the influence of shoegaze, with the debut releases by [[Cloakroom (band)|Cloakroom]], [[Narrow Head]] and Nevermind Me. The genre was popularized around 2023 by American bands Narrow Head, [[Fleshwater]] and [[Glare (band)|Glare]], and British bands [[Oversize (band)|Oversize]] and [[Split Chain]]. By 2024, it was one of the most dominant sounds in shoegaze. It was widely influential upon shoegaze in the 2020s, helping to develop the genres [[nu-gaze (2020s)|nu-gaze]] and [[zoomergaze]].
